name: TriSalus Life Sciences, Inc.
business: TriSalus Life Sciences sells the TriNav Infusion System, a medical device that uses Pressure-Enabled Drug Delivery (PEDD) technology to improve therapeutic delivery to solid tumors, primarily to hospitals and clinics for interventional radiology procedures such as transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) and radioembolization (TARE).

exposures.sentence: Our on-market, 510(k) cleared device, the TriNav Infusion System ( TriNav ) using PEDD technology is currently being used for interventional radiology procedures, most commonly transarterial radioembolization ( TARE ) and transarterial chemoembolization ( TACE ) in patients with primary liver cancer or in patients with liver metastases.

We are currently studying the ability of nelitolimod, an investigational class C TLR9 agonist, to reactivate the immune system within the liver and pancreas by broadly reprogramming immune cells and reducing myeloid derived suppressor cells ( MDSCs ), cells which cause immunosuppression, to enable more durable responses to immune checkpoint inhibitors ( CPIs ), thereby improving patient outcomes.

catalysts.event: Completion of data analysis from nelitolimod Phase I/1b studies and beginning of discussions with potential partners for Phase II development
catalysts.sentence: Results of these early trials will be available in 2026 and we will begin discussions with potential partners on further clinical development.

catalysts.event: Anticipated commercial sale of PRVI device
catalysts.sentence: Although FDA-cleared, the PRVI device has not yet been commercialized and commercial sale is not anticipated before 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 62 thousand units or $494 million market opportunity for TriNav in liver cancer TACE/TARE procedures based on current unit price of $7,983.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that 60% of these patients are eligible for TACE or TARE procedures and that between 75% and 80% are appropriate candidates for our current TriNav portfolio of devices, representing a potential market opportunity of approximately 62 thousand units, or approximately $494 million, based on our current unit price of $7,983.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market for TriSalus PEDD technology in the U.S. exceeds $2.3 billion annually.
tamClaims.sentence: The estimated total addressable market for TriSalus PEDD technology in the U.S. is project to exceed $2.3 billion annually.
